lesson: Guard every untrusted length/count field against buffer-remaining before resize/allocate, or a malicious frame becomes an allocation-DoS.
Wrap a network thread's per-frame parse/dispatch in try/catch so one malformed packet cannot std::terminate the whole host process.
On Windows, bind listener sockets with SO_EXCLUSIVEADDRUSE, not SO_REUSEADDR, so a second bind on a busy port fails loudly instead of silently co-binding and stealing traffic.
Reject non-finite (NaN/Inf) values in authoritative input (movement, physics) before they poison server state and replicate to every observer.
When multiple threads share one DB connection, put the mutex inside the connection object itself and hold it across a whole transaction span, not per-call, or BEGIN...COMMIT races corrupt rows.
Re-validate range/state gates (alive, distance, same-zone) at both action-start and action-resolution, since a target can move, flee, or die during a cast/windup between the two.
When bumping a wire/ABI protocol version, update the exporter, the consumer's static asserts, and the test's export list together in one change, or version skew fails silently at runtime.
